title: Why Intuition Requires You to Honor Your Feelings
meta_description: Discover why honoring your feelings unlocks true intuition. Stop suppressing emotions and start hearing your inner voice clearly.
og_title: Why Intuition Requires You to Honor Your Feelings
og_description: Discover why honoring your feelings unlocks true intuition. Stop suppressing emotions and start hearing your inner voice clearly.
body:
You've Been Told Your Feelings Are the Problem

Let me guess. Someone in your life - a parent, a boss, a partner, maybe even a well-meaning friend - has looked you in the eye and said something like, "You're too emotional," or "Stop being so sensitive," or the classic, "You need to toughen up."

And you believed them. For years, maybe decades, you've been trying to shut down the part of you that feels everything. You've been trying to think your way through life, make logical decisions, keep your feet on the ground, and ignore that nagging pull in your gut.

But here's the thing no one tells you: your feelings aren't the obstacle to clear intuition. They're the gateway.

You can't have one without the other. Intuition doesn't float down from some cosmic cloud as a neat little package of "do this" or "don't do that." It arrives as a feeling. A sensation. A shift in your body before your mind has even caught up. If you've been trained to ignore, suppress, or override your feelings, you've been training yourself to be deaf to your own inner guidance system.

I'm Kalesh. I teach people who feel too much how to stop treating their sensitivity like a curse and start using it as a superpower. And I'm going to be straight with you: honoring your feelings isn't about being dramatic or indulging every mood that passes through. It's about recognizing that your emotional body is the antenna through which intuition speaks. If the antenna is broken, you don't get a clear signal. Right?!

Does that land? Because if it does, you're in the right place. Let's get into why this matters so much.

The Bright Wound: Why Sensitivity Hurts Before It Heals

At Intuitives Awaken, we call this "The Bright Wound." It's the paradox of being born with a nervous system that picks up everything - the energy in a room, the unspoken tension between people, the subtle shifts in someone's voice - and having no idea what to do with it. You feel too much, so you get overwhelmed. You get overwhelmed, so you learn to shut down. You shut down, and suddenly you can't hear your intuition at all.

It's a vicious cycle. And it's not your fault.

The Bright Wound isn't a flaw. It's a sign that your system is calibrated for a wider range of input than most people's. Think of it like a radio that can pick up stations no one else can hear. That's a gift, not a curse. But if you don't know how to tune it, you just get static. And static is exhausting.

So the first step in honoring your feelings is understanding that they're not random. They're data. Your feelings are the raw material of intuition, and they're trying to tell you something real about the world around you and the choices in front of you.

"Your feelings aren't the obstacle to clear intuition. They're the gateway. You can't have one without the other."

Why Logic Alone Will Never Give You Certainty

We've been sold a story that the best decisions come from the head. Pros and cons lists. Risk assessments. Five-year plans. And sure, logic has its place. I'm not saying you should quit your job and move to a yurt based on a feeling you had during a full moon (unless that feeling is really, really clear, in which case, go for it).

But here's the problem with pure logic: it can only work with the information you already have. It can't account for the invisible. It can't factor in the energy of a person, the timing of an opportunity, or the subtle pull of a path that doesn't make sense on paper but feels right in your bones.

Intuition is the part of you that sees the whole picture - including the parts your conscious mind can't access. It's the part that knows before you know why. And it communicates through feelings.

When you ignore your feelings to make a "logical" decision, you're cutting yourself off from the most complete source of information you have. You're making a choice with one eye closed. And then you wonder why you feel uneasy, or why things don't work out the way you planned.

I've seen this happen a thousand times. Someone comes to me and says, "I made the rational choice. I took the job with more money. I stayed in the relationship that looked good on paper. I moved to the city that made the most sense. And now I feel hollow."

That hollow feeling? That's your intuition saying, "You didn't listen to me."

Honoring Feelings Doesn't Mean Being Controlled by Them

Let me clear something up before you run off and start acting on every emotion that crosses your field. Honoring your feelings is not the same as being ruled by them. There's a difference between feeling anger and acting on anger. Between noticing fear and letting fear make your decisions.

When I say "honor your feelings," I mean this: acknowledge them. Let them be there. Listen to what they're telling you. And then choose how to respond.

Think of your feelings like a weather system. You can't stop a storm from rolling in. But you can decide whether to go outside in it, wait it out, or find shelter. The storm itself isn't bad - it's just information about what's happening in the atmosphere.

Your feelings are the same. They're information about what's happening in your inner atmosphere. When you honor them, you're saying, "I see you. I hear you. Now let's figure out what to do with this information together."

This is where most people get stuck. They either suppress their feelings entirely (which kills intuition) or they let their feelings run the show (which creates chaos). The sweet spot is in the middle: feeling fully while staying grounded enough to choose your response.

One tool that helps with this is journaling. Getting your feelings out of your body and onto paper creates space between you and them. You can see them more clearly. You can start to separate the signal from the noise. If you're looking for a guided way to do this, I recommend See on Amazon (paid link) - it's a journal specifically designed to help you work through emotional patterns and connect to your inner voice.

Your Body Keeps the Score - And It's Trying to Talk to You

Here's something most people don't realize: your intuition doesn't speak in words. It speaks in sensations. A tight chest. A knot in your stomach. A sudden wave of heat. A feeling of expansion or contraction when you think about a certain person or decision.

Your body is the physical home of your intuition. And if you've spent years ignoring your feelings, you've also spent years ignoring your body. You've learned to override the signals: "I'm tired, but I'll push through." "I'm uneasy, but that's just my anxiety." "I don't want to do this, but I should."

Every time you override a signal, you tell your intuition, "Your messages don't matter." And over time, the signal gets quieter. You start to feel numb. Disconnected. Like you're going through the motions of life without really being in it.

To rebuild that connection, you have to start small. Not with big life decisions, but with tiny, everyday choices. What does your body want to eat right now? Do you actually want to go to that event, or do you feel obligated? Does this conversation feel good in your chest, or does it make you want to leave?

Start asking these questions. And more importantly, start trusting the answers. Your body is not trying to trick you. It's trying to guide you home.

The Fear Factor: Why You're Afraid to Trust Your Feelings

Let's be real for a second. The reason most people don't honor their feelings is fear. Fear of being wrong. Fear of looking stupid. Fear of making a decision based on something "unreliable" like a gut feeling.

We've been conditioned to believe that feelings are irrational, that they can't be trusted, that they'll lead us astray. And yeah, sometimes they do - if you're acting on unprocessed trauma or reactive emotion. But that's not the same as acting on clear intuitive guidance.

The difference is in the quality of the feeling. Reactive emotions are loud, urgent, and chaotic. They feel like a fire alarm going off. Intuitive feelings are quieter, steadier, and more grounded. They feel like a hand on your shoulder saying, "This way."

But here's the thing: you can't tell the difference unless you're willing to feel both. Unless you're willing to sit with the discomfort of not knowing and let the signal clarify itself.

That takes practice. It takes courage. And it takes a willingness to be wrong sometimes. Because here's the truth no one wants to admit: intuition isn't always right. But it's always trying to help.

Even when your intuition leads you down a path that doesn't work out, it's teaching you something. It's refining your signal. It's showing you what "off" feels like so you can better recognize "on."

If you're ready to start building that trust, there's a book that changed everything for me. It's called See on Amazon (paid link). It walks through exactly how to distinguish between fear-based thinking and genuine intuitive guidance. I've recommended it to dozens of students, and it's one of the most practical resources I've found.

How to Start Honoring Your Feelings Today (Without Overwhelm)

You don't need to become a meditation master or spend hours in silent retreat to start honoring your feelings. You just need to start small. Here's a simple practice that works:

Step 1: Pause before you react. The next time you feel a strong emotion - anger, sadness, excitement, anxiety - don't do anything with it. Just pause. Take a breath. Notice where it lives in your body. Is it in your chest? Your throat? Your stomach? Just notice.

Step 2: Name it. Say out loud or to yourself, "I'm feeling [emotion] right now." That's it. You don't need to fix it or change it. Just name it. This creates a tiny gap between you and the feeling, which is the beginning of choice.

Step 3: Ask what it wants. Not what it wants you to do - but what it wants you to know. Is your anger telling you a boundary has been crossed? Is your fear telling you to slow down? Is your excitement pointing toward something that matters to you? The feeling itself is the messenger. Listen to the message.

Step 4: Choose. Now that you've heard the message, you get to decide what to do with it. Maybe you act on it. Maybe you don't. But you're no longer reacting blindly. You're responding with awareness.

That's it. Four steps. Do this a few times a day, and you'll start to notice something shift. Your feelings won't feel so overwhelming. They'll feel like information. And your intuition will start to get louder because you're finally listening.

What Happens When You Honor Your Feelings Consistently

I want to paint you a picture of what life looks like on the other side of this practice. Because it's not just about making better decisions. It's about feeling alive again.

When you consistently honor your feelings, you start to trust yourself. And trust changes everything. You stop second-guessing every choice. You stop asking everyone else for their opinion. You stop needing external validation to feel okay about your decisions.

You start to move through life with a kind of quiet confidence. Not arrogance - just a knowing. A sense that even if you don't have all the answers, you trust yourself to figure it out. You trust your inner compass to point you in the right direction, even when the path isn't clear.

Your relationships change too. When you're honest with yourself about how you feel, you're able to be honest with others. You set better boundaries. You stop people-pleasing. You stop staying in situations that drain you because you're finally listening to the part of you that's been saying "this doesn't feel right" all along.

And your intuition? It becomes a trusted ally instead of a vague, unreliable whisper. You start to recognize its voice clearly. You know when it's speaking, and you know when it's just fear or old patterns. The signal gets cleaner the more you trust it.

This is the whole point. Not to become perfect. Not to never make mistakes. But to live in alignment with who you really are. And that requires feeling what you feel, without shame, without suppression, without judgment.

When You're Stuck: What to Do When the Feelings Are Too Much

I'd be lying if I said this is easy all the time. Some days, your feelings will be overwhelming. You'll feel everything at once, and you won't know what to do with it. On those days, the goal isn't to make big intuitive decisions. The goal is to ground yourself.

Grounding is the antidote to overwhelm. It brings your awareness back into your body and out of the chaos of your mind. Here's a quick grounding practice:

  • Press your feet into the floor. Feel the ground beneath you.
  • Take three slow breaths, exhaling longer than you inhale.
  • Look around the room and name five things you can see.
  • Place your hand on your heart and say, "I'm here. I'm safe."

Do this whenever the feelings feel like too much. It doesn't make the feelings go away, but it creates enough space for you to breathe. And from that space, you can start to sort through what's yours and what's not.

Because here's another thing no one tells you: not every feeling you experience is yours. As a sensitive person, you pick up other people's emotions like a sponge. That anxiety you're feeling? It might be your partner's. That heaviness? It might belong to the room you just walked into.

Learning to distinguish your feelings from others' is a key part of honoring your own. And it takes practice. But it's possible. And it's life-changing when you start to realize how much of what you've been carrying was never yours to carry.

One of the best resources I've found for this is See on Amazon (paid link). It's a practical guide to clearing energy and protecting your sensitive system - something every intuitive person needs to learn.

You're Not Broken. You're Just Learning a New Language.

I want to leave you with this: if you've spent your whole life being told there's something wrong with you for feeling too much, I'm here to tell you that's not true. You're not broken. You're not too much. You're not weak.

You're learning a new language. The language of your inner world. And like any language, it takes time to become fluent. You'll stumble. You'll misunderstand. You'll have days where you feel like you're getting nowhere.

But keep going. Because on the other side of that learning is a life where you trust yourself completely. Where your feelings guide you instead of haunt you. Where your intuition is a clear, steady voice that you know how to listen to.

That life is available to you. It starts with one simple choice: to honor what you feel, exactly as it is, right now.

And that choice? It's the most powerful one you'll ever make.

Ready to go deeper? Start by taking our free assessment to find out where your intuitive blocks are hiding. It'll show you exactly what's getting in the way of trusting yourself - and what to do about it. Take the free assessment here.
